title: Privileged Container Detection
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.1.25 docker.sock bind-mount and plaintext Engine TCP monitoring; pair that UC for daemon-level exposure. Does not evaluate Kubernetes Pod Security Admission decisions in the API server unless you enrich inspect with kube metadata. Does not perform image vulnerability scanning or supply-chain provenance. Does not cover crash loops, cgroup OOM kills, restart-policy exhaustion, or Swarm replica convergence (UC-3.1.1, UC-3.1.2, UC-3.1.13, UC-3.1.28). Skips pure containerd or CRI-O nodes without Docker-compatible inspect unless you fork the SPL. Ignores Windows Docker Desktop VMs.
evidence: Saved search container_uc_3_1_6_privileged_container_detection; lookups privileged_container_allowlist.csv and container_image_capability_baseline.csv versioned in git; weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index; dashboard drilldowns on docker:inspect HostConfig json, falco:alert payloads, and audit lines; mapping worksheets citing CIS Docker Benchmark control 5.4, MITRE ATT&CK T1611, NIST SP 800-190 section 4.5, and public container-escape CVE narratives such as CVE-2019-5736 runc overwrite and CVE-2022-0492 cgroup release_agent abuse for leadership context.

controlTest.positiveScenario: On a disposable Linux host run docker run -d --privileged --name lab-priv-probe nginx:alpine sleep 3600 without an allowlist row, ingest docker:inspect into index=containers, execute container_uc_3_1_6_privileged_container_detection, and expect critical_privileged_unapproved with allowlist_state unlisted and signal_type docker_inspect_hostconfig.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Add a privileged_container_allowlist row with matching host_id, container_name, ticket-backed approved_reason, and image_allow_regex aligned to nginx:alpine, run the same privileged container name in lab, verify inspect shows Privileged true, and confirm the saved search emits no qualifying row because allowlist_ok and image_ok suppress the alert.

dataSources: index=containers sourcetype=docker:inspect from a host-level scripted or modular input that runs docker inspect per live container id and preserves HostConfig.Privileged, HostConfig.CapAdd, HostConfig.CapDrop, HostConfig.PidMode, HostConfig.IpcMode, HostConfig.NetworkMode, HostConfig.UTSMode, HostConfig.SecurityOpt, and HostConfig.Mounts with both camelCase and snake_case aliases; index=containers sourcetype=docker:events filtered for container create and policy-bearing strings referencing privileged flags, host pid, host network, or capability additions; index=containers sourcetype=falco:alert JSON from Falco via HEC for privileged-container, dangerous capability, sensitive mount, and cgroup tamper class rules; index=os sourcetype=audit:json or sourcetype=linux_secure for auditd or secure log lines showing docker client execve with --privileged or JSON privileged true; Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux for audit transport; optional Splunk Add-on for Sysmon for Linux for enriched process lineage on dockerd; KV or CSV privileged_container_allowlist.csv exposed through transforms for join-wrapped inputlookup governance; companion lookup container_image_capability_baseline.csv for per-image expected capability posture.
app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux ([Splunkbase 833](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)) for audit:json, linux_secure, and host syslog normalization; customer-maintained docker inspect scripted input landing sourcetype=docker:inspect; Splunk Connect for Docker ([Splunkbase 3303](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3303)) or equivalent modular input for docker:events; Falco OSS with Splunk HTTP Event Collector forwarding for falco:alert; optional Splunk Add-on for Sysmon for Linux ([Splunkbase 6652](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6652)) when linux_secure lacks argv fidelity.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.1.6 Privileged Container Detection. Tunables: index=containers for docker:inspect docker:events falco:alert; index=os for audit:json or linux_secure; privileged_container_allowlist.csv keys host_id+container_name; container_image_capability_baseline.csv keys image_ref; earliest=-4h@h latest=@h; raise rolling_bad threshold via CAB if CI hosts spike.")`
| multisearch 
    [ search index=containers sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(Name, name, container_name, containerName, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(Config__Image, image, Image, "")))
      | eval created_by=toString(coalesce(created_by_user, CreatedByUser, Config__User, ""))
      | eval priv_raw=lower(toString(coalesce(host_config_privileged, HostConfig_Privileged, privileged, Privileged, "")))
      | eval privileged_flag=if(match(priv_raw,"true|1|yes") OR priv_raw="t", 1, 0)
      | eval pid_mode=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_PidMode, host_config_pid_mode, PidMode, pid_mode, "")))
      | eval ipc_mode=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_IpcMode, host_config_ipc_mode, IpcMode, ipc_mode, "")))
      | eval net_mode=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_NetworkMode, host_config_network_mode, NetworkMode, network_mode, "")))
      | eval uts_mode=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_UTSMode, host_config_uts_mode, UTSMode, uts_mode, "")))
      | eval cap_add_joined=upper(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_CapAdd, cap_add, CapAdd, cap_add_mv, "")))
      | eval cap_drop_joined=upper(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_CapDrop, cap_drop, CapDrop, cap_drop_mv, "")))
      | eval cap_add_joined=replace(replace(replace(cap_add_joined,"\"",""),"CAP_"," "),","," ")
      | eval cap_drop_joined=replace(replace(replace(cap_drop_joined,"\"",""),"CAP_"," "),","," ")
      | eval dangerous_hit=if(match(cap_add_joined,"SYS_ADMIN|SYS_MODULE|SYS_PTRACE|NET_ADMIN|NET_RAW|SYS_RAWIO|DAC_READ_SEARCH"),1,0)
      | eval risky_mount_hit=if(match(_raw,"/var/run/docker\\.sock|/var/run/crio\\.sock|/var/run/containerd/containerd\\.sock|\\\"Source\\\":\\\"/proc\\\"|\\\"Source\\\":\\\"/sys\\\"|\\\"Source\\\":\\\"/\\\"|\\\"Source\\\":\\\"/etc\\\""),1,0)
      | eval sec_opt_raw=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_SecurityOpt, host_config_security_opt, SecurityOpt, security_opt, "")))
      | eval unconfined_hit=if(match(sec_opt_raw,"apparmor[=:]unconfined|seccomp[=:]unconfined|selinux.*unconfined|label[=:]disable"),1,0)
      | eval ns_summary=strcat(
          if(like(pid_mode,"%host%"),"pid=host|",null()),
          if(like(ipc_mode,"%host%"),"ipc=host|",null()),
          if(like(net_mode,"%host%"),"net=host|",null()),
          if(like(uts_mode,"%host%"),"uts=host|",null()))
      | eval ns_summary=replace(ns_summary,"\|+$","")
      | eval signal_type="docker_inspect_hostconfig"
      | eval falco_rule=""
      | eval audit_excerpt="" ]
    [ search index=containers sourcetype="docker:events" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, "")))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"(?i)privileged|cap_add|hostpid|hostipc|hostnetwork|--cap-add|sys_admin|net_admin")
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, id, Id, actor_id, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(containerName, container_name, name, Actor__Attributes__name, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(Image, image, from, "")))
      | eval created_by=""
      | eval privileged_flag=if(match(lr,"--privileged|\\\"privileged\\\":\\s*true|hostpid"),1,0)
      | eval pid_mode=if(match(lr,"hostpid|pidmode.*host"),"host","")
      | eval ipc_mode=if(match(lr,"hostipc"),"host","")
      | eval net_mode=if(match(lr,"hostnetwork|networkmode.*host"),"host","")
      | eval uts_mode=""
      | eval cap_add_joined=if(match(lr,"cap-add|cap_add"),"EVENT_TEXT_CAPS", "")
      | eval cap_drop_joined=""
      | eval dangerous_hit=if(match(lr,"sys_admin|sys_module|sys_ptrace|net_admin|net_raw|sys_rawio|dac_read_search"),1,0)
      | eval risky_mount_hit=0
      | eval unconfined_hit=if(match(lr,"unconfined|apparmor=unconfined"),1,0)
      | eval ns_summary=strcat(
          if(pid_mode=="host","pid=host|",null()),
          if(ipc_mode=="host","ipc=host|",null()),
          if(net_mode=="host","net=host|",null()))
      | eval ns_summary=replace(ns_summary,"\|+$","")
      | eval signal_type="docker_events_policy_hint"
      | eval falco_rule=""
      | eval audit_excerpt=substr(_raw,1,512) ]
    [ search index=containers sourcetype="falco:alert" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(hostname, host, k8s_node_name, nodeName, dest, "")))
      | eval rule=toString(coalesce(rule, Rule, ruleName, ""))
      | eval rlow=lower(rule)
      | where match(rlow,"privileg|cap_sys|container\\.privileged|sensitive mount|release_agent|mount_release|dangerous.*cap|nsenter")
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerID, k8s_pod_uid, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, k8s_pod_name, containerName, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(container_image_repository, container_image_tag, image, "")))
      | eval created_by=""
      | eval privileged_flag=if(match(rlow,"privileg|privileged"),1,0)
      | eval pid_mode=""
      | eval ipc_mode=""
      | eval net_mode=""
      | eval uts_mode=""
      | eval cap_add_joined="FALCO_RULE_SIGNAL"
      | eval cap_drop_joined=""
      | eval dangerous_hit=if(match(rlow,"cap_|dangerous|module|ptrace|release_agent|mount|priv"),1,0)
      | eval risky_mount_hit=if(match(rlow,"mount|socket"),1,0)
      | eval unconfined_hit=if(match(rlow,"unconfined|apparmor"),1,0)
      | eval ns_summary=""
      | eval signal_type="falco_runtime_policy"
      | eval falco_rule=rule
      | eval audit_excerpt=toString(coalesce(output, proc_cmdline, procCmdline, evt_arg, "")) ]
    [ search index=os (sourcetype="audit:json" OR sourcetype="linux_secure") earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, dest, "")))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (match(lr,"dockerd") OR match(lr,"\\bdocker\\b")) AND match(lr,"--privileged|privileged=true|\\\"privileged\\\":\\s*true")
      | eval container_id=""
      | eval container_name="HOST_LAUNCH_SHELL"
      | eval image_ref=""
      | eval created_by=coalesce(user, USER, auid, "")
      | eval privileged_flag=1
      | eval pid_mode=""
      | eval ipc_mode=""
      | eval net_mode=""
      | eval uts_mode=""
      | eval cap_add_joined=""
      | eval cap_drop_joined=""
      | eval dangerous_hit=0
      | eval risky_mount_hit=0
      | eval unconfined_hit=0
      | eval ns_summary=""
      | eval signal_type="audit_exec_privileged_cli"
      | eval falco_rule=""
      | eval audit_excerpt=substr(_raw,1,640) ]
| eval container_name=if((isnull(container_name) OR len(trim(container_name))==0) AND len(trim(container_id))>0, container_id, container_name)
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name
    [| inputlookup privileged_container_allowlist.csv
     | eval host_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_id, host, Host, ""))))
     | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, container, name, "")))
     | eval approval=toString(coalesce(approved_reason, business_justification, ticket_id, ""))
     | eval image_allow_regex=toString(coalesce(image_allow_regex, allowed_image_regex, ""))
     | fields host_id container_name approval image_allow_regex ]
| eval allowlist_ok=if(isnotnull(approval) AND len(trim(approval))>0,1,0)
| eval image_ok=if((allowlist_ok=1) AND (len(image_allow_regex)>0) AND (len(image_ref)>0) AND match(lower(image_ref), lower(image_allow_regex)),1,if((allowlist_ok=1) AND (len(image_allow_regex)==0),1,0))
| join type=left max=0 image_ref
    [| inputlookup container_image_capability_baseline.csv
     | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(image_ref, image, Image, "")))
     | eval baseline_caps=upper(toString(coalesce(expected_cap_add_regex, baseline_cap_add, "")))
     | eval baseline_capdrop=upper(toString(coalesce(expected_cap_drop_regex, baseline_cap_drop, "")))
     | fields image_ref baseline_caps baseline_capdrop ]
| eval baseline_drift=if((len(baseline_caps)>0) AND (len(trim(cap_add_joined))>3) AND (NOT match(cap_add_joined, baseline_caps)),1,0)
| eval baseline_drop_drift=if((len(baseline_capdrop)>0) AND (
      ((NOT match(cap_drop_joined,"MKNOD")) AND match(baseline_capdrop,"MKNOD")) OR
      ((NOT match(cap_drop_joined,"AUDIT_WRITE")) AND match(baseline_capdrop,"AUDIT_WRITE")) OR
      ((NOT match(cap_drop_joined,"SYS_CHROOT")) AND match(baseline_capdrop,"SYS_CHROOT"))
    ),1,0)
| eval pid_host=if(like(pid_mode,"%host%") OR match(ns_summary,"(?i)pid=host"),1,0)
| eval other_ns_host=if(match(ns_summary,"ipc=host|net=host|uts=host"),1,0)
| eval mount_and_sec_hints=mvjoin(mvappend(
    if(risky_mount_hit=1,"risky_bind_or_runtime_socket",null()),
    if(unconfined_hit=1,"mac_profile_unconfined",null()),
    if(len(ns_summary)>0,ns_summary,null())),"|")
| eval severity_tier=case(
    allowlist_ok=1 AND image_ok=1, null(),
    allowlist_ok=1 AND image_ok=0, "high_dangerous_capability_added",
    privileged_flag=1, "critical_privileged_unapproved",
    pid_host=1, "critical_host_pid_namespace",
    baseline_drift=1 OR baseline_drop_drift=1, "medium_capability_drift_from_baseline",
    other_ns_host=1, "high_dangerous_capability_added",
    dangerous_hit=1, "high_dangerous_capability_added",
    unconfined_hit=1, "high_appsec_unconfined",
    risky_mount_hit=1, "high_dangerous_capability_added",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity_tier)
| sort 0 + host_id, container_id, _time
| eval lane_score=case(severity_tier="critical_privileged_unapproved",5, severity_tier="critical_host_pid_namespace",5, severity_tier="high_dangerous_capability_added",3, severity_tier="high_appsec_unconfined",3, severity_tier="medium_capability_drift_from_baseline",2, true(),0)
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f sum(lane_score) AS rolling_bad BY host_id, container_id
| eval sustained_drift_hits=if(rolling_bad>=9, rolling_bad, null())
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name
    [| inputlookup container_owner.csv
     | eval host_id=lower(toString(host))
     | eval container_name=toString(container_name)
     | eval owner_team=toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, ""))
     | fields host_id container_name owner_team ]
| eval allowlist_state=if(allowlist_ok=1 AND image_ok=1,"approved_active",if(allowlist_ok=1 AND image_ok=0,"approved_image_drift","unlisted"))
| table _time host_id container_id container_name image_ref signal_type created_by privileged_flag cap_add_joined mount_and_sec_hints severity_tier allowlist_state sustained_drift_hits rolling_bad baseline_caps falco_rule owner_team audit_excerpt
description: This control measures runtime privilege posture on Linux containers using declarative docker:inspect HostConfig evidence correlated with docker:events policy hints, Falco syscall-grade alerts, and host audit trails of docker clients launching --privileged workloads. It flags the privileged bit that disables default seccomp and AppArmor protections, enumerates dangerous Linux capability additions and missing baseline cap drops, and surfaces namespace sharing that collapses pid, ipc, network, or uts isolation. It also highlights bind-mount paths that recreate escape primitives against proc, sys, root, etc, or secondary runtime sockets such as crio.sock and containerd.sock, alongside mandatory access control profiles set to unconfined. Unlike UC-3.1.25, which proves whether the Docker control plane left the front door open via docker.sock or plaintext Engine TCP, this UC proves whether individual workloads were handed the keys: nearly full capability sets, host-visible process namespaces, raw networking primitives, and unconfined MAC profiles. Falco tells you when a process misbehaved; inspect tells you the container was armed for abuse before the syscall happened; audit tells you which account armed it. Together they give Fortune-500 SOCs a defensible story for incident triage, posture drift reviews, and Monday-morning executive briefings without conflating crash loops, OOM kills, or Swarm replica health signals handled elsewhere in UC-3.1.1, UC-3.1.2, UC-3.1.13, and UC-3.1.28.
value: Quantifying how often unapproved privileged containers appear, and how long they persist before someone opens a ticket, is one of the few measurable levers that predicts container-escape incident severity. Teams that only rely on runtime alerts learn about SYS_ADMIN after exploitation; teams that pair inspect snapshots with baselines learn about SYS_ADMIN at deploy time, which is the difference between a fifteen-minute image rollback and a multi-week forensics program. Regulators and enterprise customers increasingly expect evidence that container isolation boundaries are monitored, not merely promised in architecture diagrams: SOC 2 change monitoring, ISO 27001 operational security controls, and PCI DSS segmentation arguments all benefit from timestamped rows tied to allowlist governance. Mapping detections to MITRE ATT&CK T1611 gives red and blue teams a shared language when discussing blast radius. NIST SP 800-190 section 4.5 explicitly discusses container isolation expectations; this UC operationalizes that guidance with Splunk-native telemetry instead of spreadsheet attestations. Historical escapes such as CVE-2019-5736 in runc and CVE-2022-0492 involving cgroup release_agent abuse are easier to contextualize when you already know which workloads ran with effective host powers and which engineers approved that posture. Financially, one missed privileged workload can dwarf annual Splunk ingest costs for the entire docker:inspect pipeline, which makes the control rational even under aggressive FinOps pressure.
implementation: Install docker:inspect and docker:events into index=containers, Falco JSON into index=containers, and audit:json or linux_secure into index=os. Publish privileged_container_allowlist.csv, container_image_capability_baseline.csv, and container_owner.csv with lowercase host keys. Save container_uc_3_1_6_privileged_container_detection every five to fifteen minutes over earliest=-4h@h latest=@h, route critical tiers to platform and SOC jointly, archive weekly CSV snapshots with ticket ids, and keep the comment macro synchronized with approved index names.
visualization: Severity-tier ranked table by host_id with cell coloring on severity_tier, drilldown into raw docker:inspect HostConfig json and matching falco:alert output_fields; companion heatmap of dangerous capability tokens by image_ref; allowlist and baseline change timeline with annotations when privileged_container_allowlist.csv or container_image_capability_baseline.csv commits land; single-value tiles for counts of critical_privileged_unapproved and critical_host_pid_namespace in the trailing twenty-four hours; parallel line chart of rolling_bad per container_id for sustained drift reviews.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE nodename=Endpoint.Processes (Processes.process="dockerd" OR Processes.process="docker" OR Processes.process_path="*/dockerd") earliest=-24h latest=now BY Processes.dest Processes.process
| rename Processes.dest AS host | head 200
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Endpoint and Change data models on the search tier when dockerd argv, unit file, and image change events are normalized into CIM-compliant fields; retain at least ninety days of summaries for quarterly control replays.

references.title: Docker Docs — Docker Engine security overview
CIS Docker Benchmark — community program landing (Control 5.4 privileged containers)
MITRE ATT&CK — T1611 Escape to Host
NIST SP 800-190 — Application Container Security Guide
Falco Docs — Default and custom rules (privileged and capability policies)
Splunk Lantern — Docker data source guidance

knownFalsePositives: Cilium, Calico, kube-proxy, Antrea, and similar node agents sometimes require host networking or elevated capabilities that resemble policy violations until you normalize their names in privileged_container_allowlist.csv with ticket-backed approvals. NVIDIA GPU device plugins, InfiniBand helpers, and hardware performance counter exporters may need SYS_ADMIN or SYS_RAWIO on specific SKUs; document the exact image digest regex and retire approvals when vendors ship reduced-capability builds. BMC or IPMI sidecars in factory environments occasionally run privileged to touch raw devices; route those hosts to OT governance macros rather than global suppression. Ephemeral debug pods launched into non-production namespaces during incidents can trigger Falco and inspect simultaneously; require label-based CI/CD segregation so debug telemetry lands in a lower-severity routing macro. BuildKit, docker buildx, or Kaniko-adjacent builders that still use --privileged for nested virtualization will fire on shared CI executors; maintain parallel allowlists keyed by host class or builder pool names. Service mesh init containers that mount cgroup or proc paths for observability can resemble risky bind hints; corroborate with service mesh vendor documentation before muting. Falco rules that broadly match sensitive mount can flag read-only library paths during legitimate JVM diagnostics; tune only after docker:inspect proves mounts are expected. Audit pipelines that log every docker pull can interleave argv fragments that look like privileged launches; require multi-token matches before paging. Rolling kernel upgrades can reset capability baseline hashes briefly; reconcile container_image_capability_baseline.csv when golden images move. Mirantis Container Runtime field naming differences after upgrades can cause coalesce misses that look like drift until props aliases are refreshed.

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: optional — risk notables or RBA correlation when privilege drift aligns with rare images, socket exposure from UC-3.1.25, or lateral movement analytics

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the container security engineering lead and the SOC detection owner who signs off on alert fidelity. Treat it as a production gate for any estate where Docker Engine still supervises workloads directly, where Kubernetes nodes run a Docker shim, or where CI systems spawn privileged builders. This document assumes Linux hosts with cgroup v1 or unified cgroup v2 hierarchies, auditd or an equivalent audit pipeline, and a Falco deployment that can emit JSON alerts to Splunk HTTP Event Collector without stripping output_fields. UC-3.1.25 remains the authoritative detector for Docker control-socket bind mounts and plaintext Engine TCP listeners; this UC answers a different question, namely whether individual containers are configured with runtime privileges that collapse namespace boundaries, expand Linux capabilities into kernel-equivalent powers, or strip mandatory access control profiles even when the daemon socket is not mounted inside the workload.

Before you index telemetry, inventory the scripted input or modular input that materializes sourcetype docker:inspect. The collector account must be able to run docker inspect against every live container id on a cadence your change board accepts, typically five to fifteen minutes on static production nodes and faster on CI executors where ephemeral tasks might exist for only seconds. Field fidelity matters more than raw prettiness: HostConfig.Privileged, HostConfig.CapAdd, HostConfig.CapDrop, HostConfig.PidMode, HostConfig.IpcMode, HostConfig.NetworkMode, HostConfig.UTSMode, HostConfig.SecurityOpt, and HostConfig.Mounts must survive props transforms with both camelCase and snake_case aliases so the SPL coalesce lists stay short. If your team flattens inspect JSON with a forwarder-side Python helper, document the exact keys it emits and keep sample events in a restricted evidence index for quarterly replay tests.

Governance lookups live beside the saved search. privileged_container_allowlist.csv carries host_id, container_name, approved_reason, and optional image_allow_regex for digest-pinning approved platform agents such as kube-proxy, cilium-node, nvidia-device-plugin, and hardware telemetry sidecars. container_image_capability_baseline.csv lists image_ref plus expected_cap_add_regex and expected_cap_drop_regex strings that describe the approved capability posture per golden image; refresh it when base images change. container_owner.csv continues the UC-3.1.x pattern for pager routing. If you store allowlists in KV Store collections, expose them through transforms.conf names that still work with inputlookup inside join subsearches so Cloud search heads and on-prem search heads share identical SPL.

Risk framing for executives: the privileged bit is not a cosmetic warning. It grants the container almost every capability bit, relaxes seccomp and AppArmor defaults, and exposes host devices in ways that turn a single memory corruption bug into a host-root compromise. Capability additions such as SYS_ADMIN or SYS_MODULE let an attacker load kernel code or manipulate cgroups in ways that bypass namespace isolation, including historical abuse of cgroup release_agent paths. Host pid namespaces let a container observe and signal every process on the machine. Host network namespaces sidestep network segmentation assumptions encoded in service meshes and cloud security groups. Bind-mounting proc, sys, the root filesystem, or secondary container runtime sockets recreates many of the escape primitives that UC-3.1.25 tracks at the docker.sock layer, but this UC catches them even when the primary Docker socket is not present inside the container. Falco supplies syscall-grade corroboration; docker:inspect supplies declarative truth; audit captures who launched a privileged container from the shell.

Differentiation recap relative to sibling UCs: UC-3.1.1, UC-3.1.2, and UC-3.1.13 monitor crash loops, cgroup OOM kills, and restart-policy cadence without reading HostConfig security posture. UC-3.1.28 covers Swarm replica convergence. UC-3.1.25 focuses on daemon-level socket exposure and unauthenticated Engine listeners. UC-3.1.6 is the runtime privilege configuration story on the workload object itself. Do not merge those concerns into one alert or analysts will lose the ability to route socket incidents to infrastructure firewall owners while routing SYS_ADMIN drift to application security.

Licensing and volume: inspect snapshots are larger than docker:events alone. Cap arrays and Mounts arrays multiply bytes. Compress at collection, strip indentation, and consider summarizing historical inspect into a metrics index after raw retention closes. Falco alert volume scales with rule breadth; keep privileged-container rules in enforce mode but tune nested macros for known hardware agents only after allowlist entries exist.

Legal and privacy: inspect events can include environment variable fragments if your collector is careless. Redact secrets at the forwarder before HEC. Restrict indexes to roles that need HostConfig detail.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Deploy docker:inspect collection first because it anchors declarative truth. On each Linux worker running Docker CE, Mirantis Container Runtime, or an approved enterprise Engine build, install a privileged systemd timer or Splunk modular input that enumerates docker ps -q, runs docker inspect -f json or equivalent bulk JSON for each id, and posts one HEC event per container with sourcetype docker:inspect. Normalize host_id to the same lowercase short name Universal Forwarders use in their host field. Include container_id, image reference, command, and created_by_user when available so SOC playbooks can pivot to CI job metadata without re-querying the daemon during incidents.

Configure Splunk Connect for Docker or your events modular input to stream docker:events into the same index with filters that retain container create, destroy, and exec-adjacent lines that include capability or privileged strings. Preserve Actor attribute keys for Compose and Kubernetes labels when your estate mixes orchestrators; those labels become invaluable when container_name alone is ambiguous across namepaces.

Install Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux on forwarders that can read auditd logs. Map audit:json or linux_secure sourcetypes depending on whether you use audisp-json plugins or classic syslog bridging. Add audit rules that record execve for docker client binaries with argv containing double-dash privileged, and retain dockerd restarts that reveal dangerous defaults. Optional Splunk Add-on for Sysmon for Linux enriches lineage when dockerd is relaunched from systemd overrides; treat it as additive evidence, not a replacement for auditd coverage of user-driven docker run commands.

Point Falco at the same hosts with rules that flag privileged containers, sensitive capability sets, suspicious mounts, and cgroup release_agent tamper patterns. Forward falco:alert JSON with output_fields that include container_id, container_image_repository, proc_cmdline, evt_type, and fd names when sockets are involved. Keep HEC tokens in vault and rotate quarterly.

Expected validation searches before alert authoring: index=containers sourcetype=docker:inspect earliest=-15m showing HostConfig fragments, index=containers sourcetype=falco:alert rule=*priv* earliest=-24h, index=os sourcetype=audit:json OR linux_secure docker earliest=-24h. Skew between host clock and Splunk _time must stay under thirty seconds or streamstats sustained gates will lie.

Step 3 — Create search and alert

Save the SPL as container_uc_3_1_6_privileged_container_detection with schedule every five or fifteen minutes and earliest=-4h@h latest=@h during steady state; tighten to one hour during cost reviews if Job Inspector shows heavy scan volume. Throttle duplicate critical_privileged_unapproved rows per host_id and container_name for forty-five minutes unless rolling_bad jumps by more than six points inside an hour, which often indicates automated exploitation or a broken CI template repaving pods repeatedly.

Understanding the pipeline: the opening comment macro is the operator contract for index names, lookup paths, and governance thresholds. multisearch fans four arms so a silent failure in Falco does not hide inspect truth, and audit captures shell launches that never touched the Falco BPF probe. coalesce lists absorb camelCase and snake_case vendor differences. The first join wraps privileged_container_allowlist.csv using type=left max=0 to avoid lookup explosions. The second join layers per-image capability baselines so medium_capability_drift_from_baseline represents policy drift rather than a one-off parser glitch. pid_host and other_ns_host gates keep critical_host_pid_namespace focused on PID namespace sharing while still elevating ipc, net, and uts host breaks into high_dangerous_capability_added. streamstats sums lane_score across ordered events per host_id and container_id to expose sustained_drift_hits when rolling_bad crosses nine, signaling repeated privilege anomalies across consecutive polls rather than a single transient sample. The closing join to container_owner.csv routes pages without using bare lookup commands for the allowlist path.

Severity semantics: critical_privileged_unapproved means HostConfig.Privileged is true and the workload lacks a matching allowlist row with aligned image regex. critical_host_pid_namespace means the container shares the host PID namespace. high_dangerous_capability_added covers dangerous capability adds, non-PID host namespace breaks, risky bind hints surfaced from inspect raw matching, unapproved image drift on an allowlisted name, and Falco policy hits that imply capability or mount abuse. high_appsec_unconfined means AppArmor or SELinux profiles were explicitly set to unconfined or seccomp unconfined equivalents. medium_capability_drift_from_baseline means capability add or drop posture diverged from the signed baseline for that image. Analysts should not downgrade medium rows without reconciling the baseline CSV.

Alert actions: route critical tiers to platform secops joint bridge, attach audit_excerpt when signal_type equals audit_exec_privileged_cli, and include Falco json deep links for falco_runtime_policy rows. Medium tiers should open tickets automatically but may skip voice pages unless sustained_drift_hits is non-null.

Fenced SPL for runbooks must match the spl JSON field byte-for-byte:

```spl
`comment("UC-3.1.6 Privileged Container Detection. Tunables: index=containers for docker:inspect docker:events falco:alert; index=os for audit:json or linux_secure; privileged_container_allowlist.csv keys host_id+container_name; container_image_capability_baseline.csv keys image_ref; earliest=-4h@h latest=@h; raise rolling_bad threshold via CAB if CI hosts spike.")`
| multisearch 
    [ search index=containers sourcetype="docker:inspect" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, dest, "")))
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, Id, id, containerId, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(Name, name, container_name, containerName, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(Config__Image, image, Image, "")))
      | eval created_by=toString(coalesce(created_by_user, CreatedByUser, Config__User, ""))
      | eval priv_raw=lower(toString(coalesce(host_config_privileged, HostConfig_Privileged, privileged, Privileged, "")))
      | eval privileged_flag=if(match(priv_raw,"true|1|yes") OR priv_raw="t", 1, 0)
      | eval pid_mode=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_PidMode, host_config_pid_mode, PidMode, pid_mode, "")))
      | eval ipc_mode=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_IpcMode, host_config_ipc_mode, IpcMode, ipc_mode, "")))
      | eval net_mode=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_NetworkMode, host_config_network_mode, NetworkMode, network_mode, "")))
      | eval uts_mode=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_UTSMode, host_config_uts_mode, UTSMode, uts_mode, "")))
      | eval cap_add_joined=upper(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_CapAdd, cap_add, CapAdd, cap_add_mv, "")))
      | eval cap_drop_joined=upper(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_CapDrop, cap_drop, CapDrop, cap_drop_mv, "")))
      | eval cap_add_joined=replace(replace(replace(cap_add_joined,"\"",""),"CAP_"," "),","," ")
      | eval cap_drop_joined=replace(replace(replace(cap_drop_joined,"\"",""),"CAP_"," "),","," ")
      | eval dangerous_hit=if(match(cap_add_joined,"SYS_ADMIN|SYS_MODULE|SYS_PTRACE|NET_ADMIN|NET_RAW|SYS_RAWIO|DAC_READ_SEARCH"),1,0)
      | eval risky_mount_hit=if(match(_raw,"/var/run/docker\\.sock|/var/run/crio\\.sock|/var/run/containerd/containerd\\.sock|\\\"Source\\\":\\\"/proc\\\"|\\\"Source\\\":\\\"/sys\\\"|\\\"Source\\\":\\\"/\\\"|\\\"Source\\\":\\\"/etc\\\""),1,0)
      | eval sec_opt_raw=lower(toString(coalesce(HostConfig_SecurityOpt, host_config_security_opt, SecurityOpt, security_opt, "")))
      | eval unconfined_hit=if(match(sec_opt_raw,"apparmor[=:]unconfined|seccomp[=:]unconfined|selinux.*unconfined|label[=:]disable"),1,0)
      | eval ns_summary=strcat(
          if(like(pid_mode,"%host%"),"pid=host|",null()),
          if(like(ipc_mode,"%host%"),"ipc=host|",null()),
          if(like(net_mode,"%host%"),"net=host|",null()),
          if(like(uts_mode,"%host%"),"uts=host|",null()))
      | eval ns_summary=replace(ns_summary,"\|+$","")
      | eval signal_type="docker_inspect_hostconfig"
      | eval falco_rule=""
      | eval audit_excerpt="" ]
    [ search index=containers sourcetype="docker:events" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, host_id, "")))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"(?i)privileged|cap_add|hostpid|hostipc|hostnetwork|--cap-add|sys_admin|net_admin")
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, id, Id, actor_id, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(containerName, container_name, name, Actor__Attributes__name, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(Image, image, from, "")))
      | eval created_by=""
      | eval privileged_flag=if(match(lr,"--privileged|\\\"privileged\\\":\\s*true|hostpid"),1,0)
      | eval pid_mode=if(match(lr,"hostpid|pidmode.*host"),"host","")
      | eval ipc_mode=if(match(lr,"hostipc"),"host","")
      | eval net_mode=if(match(lr,"hostnetwork|networkmode.*host"),"host","")
      | eval uts_mode=""
      | eval cap_add_joined=if(match(lr,"cap-add|cap_add"),"EVENT_TEXT_CAPS", "")
      | eval cap_drop_joined=""
      | eval dangerous_hit=if(match(lr,"sys_admin|sys_module|sys_ptrace|net_admin|net_raw|sys_rawio|dac_read_search"),1,0)
      | eval risky_mount_hit=0
      | eval unconfined_hit=if(match(lr,"unconfined|apparmor=unconfined"),1,0)
      | eval ns_summary=strcat(
          if(pid_mode=="host","pid=host|",null()),
          if(ipc_mode=="host","ipc=host|",null()),
          if(net_mode=="host","net=host|",null()))
      | eval ns_summary=replace(ns_summary,"\|+$","")
      | eval signal_type="docker_events_policy_hint"
      | eval falco_rule=""
      | eval audit_excerpt=substr(_raw,1,512) ]
    [ search index=containers sourcetype="falco:alert" earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(hostname, host, k8s_node_name, nodeName, dest, "")))
      | eval rule=toString(coalesce(rule, Rule, ruleName, ""))
      | eval rlow=lower(rule)
      | where match(rlow,"privileg|cap_sys|container\\.privileged|sensitive mount|release_agent|mount_release|dangerous.*cap|nsenter")
      | eval container_id=trim(toString(coalesce(container_id, containerID, k8s_pod_uid, "")))
      | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, k8s_pod_name, containerName, "")))
      | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(container_image_repository, container_image_tag, image, "")))
      | eval created_by=""
      | eval privileged_flag=if(match(rlow,"privileg|privileged"),1,0)
      | eval pid_mode=""
      | eval ipc_mode=""
      | eval net_mode=""
      | eval uts_mode=""
      | eval cap_add_joined="FALCO_RULE_SIGNAL"
      | eval cap_drop_joined=""
      | eval dangerous_hit=if(match(rlow,"cap_|dangerous|module|ptrace|release_agent|mount|priv"),1,0)
      | eval risky_mount_hit=if(match(rlow,"mount|socket"),1,0)
      | eval unconfined_hit=if(match(rlow,"unconfined|apparmor"),1,0)
      | eval ns_summary=""
      | eval signal_type="falco_runtime_policy"
      | eval falco_rule=rule
      | eval audit_excerpt=toString(coalesce(output, proc_cmdline, procCmdline, evt_arg, "")) ]
    [ search index=os (sourcetype="audit:json" OR sourcetype="linux_secure") earliest=-4h@h latest=@h
      | eval host_id=lower(toString(coalesce(host, Host, hostname, dest, "")))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where (match(lr,"dockerd") OR match(lr,"\\bdocker\\b")) AND match(lr,"--privileged|privileged=true|\\\"privileged\\\":\\s*true")
      | eval container_id=""
      | eval container_name="HOST_LAUNCH_SHELL"
      | eval image_ref=""
      | eval created_by=coalesce(user, USER, auid, "")
      | eval privileged_flag=1
      | eval pid_mode=""
      | eval ipc_mode=""
      | eval net_mode=""
      | eval uts_mode=""
      | eval cap_add_joined=""
      | eval cap_drop_joined=""
      | eval dangerous_hit=0
      | eval risky_mount_hit=0
      | eval unconfined_hit=0
      | eval ns_summary=""
      | eval signal_type="audit_exec_privileged_cli"
      | eval falco_rule=""
      | eval audit_excerpt=substr(_raw,1,640) ]
| eval container_name=if((isnull(container_name) OR len(trim(container_name))==0) AND len(trim(container_id))>0, container_id, container_name)
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name
    [| inputlookup privileged_container_allowlist.csv
     | eval host_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host_id, host, Host, ""))))
     | eval container_name=trim(toString(coalesce(container_name, container, name, "")))
     | eval approval=toString(coalesce(approved_reason, business_justification, ticket_id, ""))
     | eval image_allow_regex=toString(coalesce(image_allow_regex, allowed_image_regex, ""))
     | fields host_id container_name approval image_allow_regex ]
| eval allowlist_ok=if(isnotnull(approval) AND len(trim(approval))>0,1,0)
| eval image_ok=if((allowlist_ok=1) AND (len(image_allow_regex)>0) AND (len(image_ref)>0) AND match(lower(image_ref), lower(image_allow_regex)),1,if((allowlist_ok=1) AND (len(image_allow_regex)==0),1,0))
| join type=left max=0 image_ref
    [| inputlookup container_image_capability_baseline.csv
     | eval image_ref=trim(toString(coalesce(image_ref, image, Image, "")))
     | eval baseline_caps=upper(toString(coalesce(expected_cap_add_regex, baseline_cap_add, "")))
     | eval baseline_capdrop=upper(toString(coalesce(expected_cap_drop_regex, baseline_cap_drop, "")))
     | fields image_ref baseline_caps baseline_capdrop ]
| eval baseline_drift=if((len(baseline_caps)>0) AND (len(trim(cap_add_joined))>3) AND (NOT match(cap_add_joined, baseline_caps)),1,0)
| eval baseline_drop_drift=if((len(baseline_capdrop)>0) AND (
      ((NOT match(cap_drop_joined,"MKNOD")) AND match(baseline_capdrop,"MKNOD")) OR
      ((NOT match(cap_drop_joined,"AUDIT_WRITE")) AND match(baseline_capdrop,"AUDIT_WRITE")) OR
      ((NOT match(cap_drop_joined,"SYS_CHROOT")) AND match(baseline_capdrop,"SYS_CHROOT"))
    ),1,0)
| eval pid_host=if(like(pid_mode,"%host%") OR match(ns_summary,"(?i)pid=host"),1,0)
| eval other_ns_host=if(match(ns_summary,"ipc=host|net=host|uts=host"),1,0)
| eval mount_and_sec_hints=mvjoin(mvappend(
    if(risky_mount_hit=1,"risky_bind_or_runtime_socket",null()),
    if(unconfined_hit=1,"mac_profile_unconfined",null()),
    if(len(ns_summary)>0,ns_summary,null())),"|")
| eval severity_tier=case(
    allowlist_ok=1 AND image_ok=1, null(),
    allowlist_ok=1 AND image_ok=0, "high_dangerous_capability_added",
    privileged_flag=1, "critical_privileged_unapproved",
    pid_host=1, "critical_host_pid_namespace",
    baseline_drift=1 OR baseline_drop_drift=1, "medium_capability_drift_from_baseline",
    other_ns_host=1, "high_dangerous_capability_added",
    dangerous_hit=1, "high_dangerous_capability_added",
    unconfined_hit=1, "high_appsec_unconfined",
    risky_mount_hit=1, "high_dangerous_capability_added",
    true(), null())
| where isnotnull(severity_tier)
| sort 0 + host_id, container_id, _time
| eval lane_score=case(severity_tier="critical_privileged_unapproved",5, severity_tier="critical_host_pid_namespace",5, severity_tier="high_dangerous_capability_added",3, severity_tier="high_appsec_unconfined",3, severity_tier="medium_capability_drift_from_baseline",2, true(),0)
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f sum(lane_score) AS rolling_bad BY host_id, container_id
| eval sustained_drift_hits=if(rolling_bad>=9, rolling_bad, null())
| join type=left max=0 host_id, container_name
    [| inputlookup container_owner.csv
     | eval host_id=lower(toString(host))
     | eval container_name=toString(container_name)
     | eval owner_team=toString(coalesce(owner_team, squad, ""))
     | fields host_id container_name owner_team ]
| eval allowlist_state=if(allowlist_ok=1 AND image_ok=1,"approved_active",if(allowlist_ok=1 AND image_ok=0,"approved_image_drift","unlisted"))
| table _time host_id container_id container_name image_ref signal_type created_by privileged_flag cap_add_joined mount_and_sec_hints severity_tier allowlist_state sustained_drift_hits rolling_bad baseline_caps falco_rule owner_team audit_excerpt
```

Step 4 — Validate

Positive test A — privileged nginx: on a disposable lab host run docker run -d --privileged --name lab-priv-nginx nginx:alpine sleep 3600 without an allowlist row. Wait for inspect ingestion, execute the saved search, and expect critical_privileged_unapproved with signal_type docker_inspect_hostconfig and allowlist_state unlisted. Remove the container immediately after capture.

Positive test B — host pid namespace: run docker run -d --pid=host --name lab-pidhost alpine:3.19 sleep 3600. Expect critical_host_pid_namespace even though privileged may be false. Document why this is equivalent risk for process visibility.

Positive test C — capability add: run docker run -d --cap-add=SYS_ADMIN --name lab-cap alpine:3.19 sleep 3600. Expect high_dangerous_capability_added from inspect with cap_add_joined containing SYS_ADMIN. Pair with a baseline row that forbids SYS_ADMIN to see medium_capability_drift_from_baseline when baseline logic triggers.

Positive test D — Falco: trigger a ruled privileged workload in lab per vendor guidance. Expect falco_runtime_policy lane with non-null falco_rule.

Positive test E — audit shell: run docker run --privileged hello-world from an audited shell account. Expect audit_exec_privileged_cli with non-null audit_excerpt.

Negative test — allowlisted kube-proxy mimic: add a privileged_container_allowlist row with ticket-backed approved_reason and image_allow_regex matching your lab image, run a matching container, confirm zero severity rows after joins because allowlist_ok and image_ok suppress the alert.

Correlation test: confirm docker:events hints arrive within one minute of inspect for the same container id. If not, tighten poll intervals before trusting events-only triage.

RBAC test: readers without index=containers must see no results, proving container intelligence stays gated.

Performance test: run Job Inspector during Monday peaks; if scan cost exceeds SRE budget, push inspect summaries into a dedicated summary index retaining only privilege flags and cap hashes.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Inspect arm empty but Falco fires: inspect poller may have lost docker.sock permissions or the container died between polls. Verify collector group membership, systemd status, and splunkd.log REST errors. Remediation is fixing the input, not muting Falco.

Case 2 — Allowlist join misses due to Compose prefixes: normalize container_name in the CSV publisher to include project prefixes exactly as docker:events prints them, or add alias rows per stack.

Case 3 — Baseline drift false positives after image promotion: refresh container_image_capability_baseline.csv with the new digest regex and attach a change ticket hash to the commit.

Case 4 — streamstats rolling_bad resets every search: container_id may change while container_name stays stable on rolling recreate; add a secondary key using image_ref plus service label if your TA exposes it.

Case 5 — audit arm floods during patch Tuesday: systemd restarts can resemble mass privileged launches; require docker argv matches beyond generic dockerd strings before paging.

Case 6 — Falco duplicates inspect hits: keep both signals for evidence; deduplicate in presentation dashboards using stats latest by host_id and container_id.

Case 7 — Critical PID namespace rows from service mesh init containers: validate whether init truly requires host PID; if yes, allowlist with extreme scrutiny and quarterly review.

Case 8 — Risky mount regex misses custom graph driver paths: extend the match list in the inspect arm with change-board approval when new runtime sockets appear.

Case 9 — rolling_bad never reaches nine on bursty CI hosts: lower the sustained threshold in the comment macro for those host classes or summarize per fifteen-minute buckets upstream.

Case 10 — image_allow_regex too broad: tighten patterns to registry/repo with version anchors; reject wildcards that defeat drift detection.

Case 11 — Kubernetes mirror pods skew names: enrich docker:inspect with pod label fields when the scripted input can read them, or maintain a secondary join table mapping container id to kube metadata.

Case 12 — cgroup v2-only hosts omit legacy cap strings: confirm your flattening script still emits CAP_ normalized tokens the dangerous_hit regex expects.

Dashboard publishing: follow the visualization field with severity-tier coloring, drilldowns to raw inspect JSON, and annotations when privileged_container_allowlist.csv changes.

Evidence retention: weekly CSV exports of the closing table to a restricted index with lookup commit hashes satisfy most SOC2, ISO 27001, and PCI DSS control testers when paired with tickets.

Governance: quarterly replay one historical incident through the SPL after Docker Engine or Falco upgrades, and update the comment macro when indexes move.

Closing checklist: five step headers use plain text with em dashes, multisearch lists four arms, coalesce appears throughout, join wraps both privileged_container_allowlist.csv and container_image_capability_baseline.csv, streamstats implements sustained scoring, case emits only the five mandated severity strings plus null for approved rows, table projects nineteen analyst columns, narrative JSON fields avoid forbidden boilerplate phrases, and references span Docker docs, CIS guidance, MITRE T1611, NIST SP 800-190, Falco rules documentation, and Splunk Lantern Docker onboarding depth.

Supplemental engineering notes for long-term owners: when rootless Docker becomes default, inspect paths remain valid but capability semantics shift; revalidate dangerous_hit lists with vendor guidance. When migrating nodes to pure containerd without Docker, retire this UC on those classes rather than silencing detections. When integrating Splunk Enterprise Security, map severity_tier to risk scores with highest weight on critical_privileged_unapproved. When legal requests litigation holds, include docker:inspect json, Falco output_fields, and matching audit lines in the preservation scope. When finance questions license cost, compare inspect volume to the expected cost of a single container-escape incident response retainer. When red teaming, pair this UC with UC-3.1.25 to tell a full story about socket gates versus runtime privilege. When OT gateways run Docker at the edge, duplicate governance lookups with OT-specific owners. When service meshes inject privileged init containers, document mesh vendor requirements before approving allowlists. When vulnerability management publishes new runc or kernel CVEs, rehearse this alert against lab reproductions to confirm signals still fire. When board decks need a single sentence, describe runtime privilege as handing workload code the same powers as a hypervisor admin, not as a vague compliance checkbox.
grandmaExplanation: We check whether little boxed programs were accidentally given master keys to the whole computer—like seeing every process on the machine, loading deep kernel modules, or turning off the safety profiles that keep programs contained. When that happens without paperwork, we sound the alarm so a small break-in cannot become a takeover.
hardware: Targets x86_64 and ARM64 Linux workers running Docker CE, Mirantis Container Runtime, or enterprise-hardened Engine builds on fleet SKUs such as Dell PowerEdge R750, HPE ProLiant DL360 Gen11, Lenovo ThinkSystem SR650, and AWS Graviton instances including m7g families where cgroup paths remain Linux-standard. Nodes must expose auditd or an audisp-json equivalent and maintain accurate time sync for streamstats ordering. Edge industrial gateways that still embed Docker should follow the same inspect cadence when regulation marks them in-scope. Windows containers and pure serverless estates without local dockerd are explicitly out of scope for this control’s default SPL.
